page: Microsoft Azure E8d_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 307 GB of local SSD storage, while lacking any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and database workloads. The processor supports AVX-512 vector extensions, contributing to average scores in Passmark CPU extended instructions (7200.09) and stress-ng multi-core tests (7432.24). Redis SET operations perform at an average rate of 6.53 million operations per second, and Passmark database operations score 2917.65. However, the system exhibits performance bottlenecks under memory-intensive and large-block operations. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths are weak, measuring 28.37 GB/sec and 22.01 GB/sec respectively. Similarly, Geekbench single-core (1064.0) and multi-core (4065.0) scores fall into the bottom 25% tier.

Given the high memory density of 8 GB per vCPU and the inclusion of 307 GB local storage, the E8d_v4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require top-tier CPU compute speeds. It is well-suited for relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and moderate data processing tasks. However, it is poorly suited for large language model inference, with medium 7B model prompt processing dropping to a poor 11.38 tokens per second, and is not recommended for high-throughput static web serving or heavy image processing.
description: The Microsoft Azure E8d_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDv4 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It includes 307 GB of local storage but lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show average performance in database operations (2917.65 on Passmark) and Redis SET operations (6.53 million operations/sec), supported by AVX-512 instruction sets. However, it exhibits weak performance in large-block memory bandwidth (28.37 GB/sec read) and Geekbench multi-core scores (4065.0). Qualitatively, this instance offers a balanced cost-to-resource profile for memory-intensive workloads that require local scratch space but do not demand high-performance computing. It is best suited for relational databases, caching layers, and moderate data processing, while being poorly suited for LLM inference or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8d_v4 is an EDv4 family virtual machine with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, offering balanced memory-optimized performance for databases and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local SSD storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8 GB per vCPU ratio
307 GB of local SSD storage for fast scratch space and caching
Average database performance with 6.53 million Redis SET operations per second
Weak large-block memory bandwidth and low LLM inference speeds
Best suited for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications
